equalizers.title: Equalizer:
equalizers.peakLimiterTooltip:
Enable if you hear Distortion when increasing Gain.
This will decrease the Global Gain 
 to make sure the sound never clips.
Then increase the overall volume to compensate
expertEq.selectBandHint: Select a band on the graph or add one to edit its parameters
autoeq.title: AutoEQ Headphone Presets
presets.defaultNameExists: A Default preset with this name already exists. Would you like to use this name anyway? You might see Duplicate names in the Preset list.
presets.nameExists: A preset with this name already exists. Would you like to overwrite it?
presets.removeConfirmation: Are you sure you want to remove this Preset?
recorder.revealLast: Show the last recording in Finder
recorder.resetFolder: Reset to the default recordings folder

hearingTest.title: Hearing Test
hearingTest.requiresNewerVersion: The Hearing Test requires eqMac version {version} or newer. Please update eqMac.
hearingTest.intro: This test estimates the quietest tone you can hear at {count} frequencies per ear, then builds a personalized Equalizer preset that compensates for the differences.
hearingTest.calibrationWarning: Volume calibration: put on headphones and set your system volume to a comfortable, moderate level BEFORE starting — then do not touch it during the test. Otherwise tones may be uncomfortably loud, or the results meaningless.
hearingTest.quietRoomHint: Sit in a quiet room. The Equalizer is temporarily disabled during the test and restored when it finishes. This is not a medical hearing test — results are only for audio personalization.
hearingTest.toneInstructions: A tone is playing. Press "I can hear it" while it is audible — it will get quieter. Press "I can't hear it" once it disappears.
hearingTest.resultsIntro: Your compensation curve, relative to each ear's 1 kHz threshold:
hearingTest.flatHint: Your thresholds are well balanced across the tested range — the compensation curve is nearly flat.
hearingTest.saveHint: Save the result as an Equalizer preset: Expert keeps separate left / right ear correction, Advanced is a simpler 10-band approximation applied to both channels.
hearingTest.interrupted: The Hearing Test was interrupted by an audio pipeline change. Please start again.
hearingTest.enterPresetName: Please enter a name for the Preset
hearingTest.savedExpert: Saved. Switch the Equalizer to Expert mode and select the preset to use it.
hearingTest.savedAdvanced: Saved. Switch the Equalizer to Advanced mode and select the preset to use it.
hotkeys.title: Global Hotkeys
hotkeys.requiresNewerVersion: Global Hotkeys require eqMac version {version} or newer. Please update eqMac.
hotkeys.hint: Click Record, then press a key combination. Press Esc to cancel. Hotkeys work system wide, even while eqMac is in the background.
midi.title: MIDI Controls
midi.requiresNewerVersion: MIDI Control requires a newer version of eqMac
midi.learnTooltip: Bind the next incoming CC / Note to this action
midi.noMappings: No mappings yet. Pick an action below and press Learn.
superPresets.title: Super Presets
superPresets.requiresNewerVersion: Super Presets require a newer version of the eqMac app. Please update eqMac to use this feature.
superPresets.hint: Switch to a Preset when an Output Device is selected or an App comes to the front. App rules take priority over Device rules.
superPresets.noRules: No rules yet. Add a rule to get started.
superPresets.revertTooltip: Restore the previous Preset when this trigger stops matching
arrangement.reorderHint: Reorder the main window sections and choose which ones are visible.
arrangement.hiddenHint: Hidden sections keep their settings and continue to process audio.
settings.telemetryTooltip:
eqMac would collect anonymous Telemetry analytics data like:

• macOS Version
• App and UI Version
• Country (IP Addresses are anonymized)

This helps us understand distribution of our users.
settings.crashReportsTooltip:
eqMac would send anonymized crash reports
back to the developer in case eqMac crashes.
This helps us understand improve eqMac 
and make it a more stable product.
settings.otaUpdatesTooltip:
Because eqMac's User Interface is built with Web Technologies 
the developer can periodically push Over the Air (OTA) updates,
make minor bug fixes and UI improvements,
all without needing the user to do a full app update.
settings.betaProgramTooltip:
Get and test the most latest changes to eqMac.
Help the developer identify and fix uncaught issues, 
before they go out to all users.
themes.pickerHint: Click a theme to apply it instantly, or drag the hue slider to override the accent color.
privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.intro:
eqMac respects its user's privacy 
and is giving you a choice what data you wish to share with the developer.
This data would help us improve and grow the product.
privacy.analyticsTooltip:
eqMac would collect anonymous Telemetry analytics data like:

• macOS Version
• App and UI Version
• Country (IP Addresses are anonymized)

This helps us understand distribution of our users.
privacy.crashReportsTooltip:
eqMac would send anonymized crash reports
back to the developer in case eqMac crashes.
This helps us understand improve eqMac 
and make it a more stable product.
